items.fix: Check res.ok (or res.status) and throw yourself.
items.why: fetch only rejects on network errors; HTTP errors still resolve.

items.mistake: Forgetting Content-Type or not stringifying the body.
items.why: The server can't parse the payload otherwise.

items.mistake: Calling a key-protected API from browser code with the key embedded.
items.fix: Proxy through a backend that holds the key server-side.
items.why: Client-side keys are visible to users and get stolen.
items.example: Browser: `fetch('/api/chat', {body: ...})` → your Node `/api/chat` → calls OpenAI with `Authorization: Bearer ${process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY}`. Key never reaches the browser.

items.mistake: No timeout, so a hung request blocks indefinitely.
items.fix: Use an AbortController with a setTimeout.
items.example:
```ts
const c = new AbortController()
setTimeout(() => c.abort(), 5000)
await fetch(url, { signal: c.signal })   // throws AbortError after 5s
```
